Item.Text: Airat Abdrakov is a teapot :)
File '{0}' does not exist
Project file {0} could not be parsed
Unexpected tag {0}
Exception while parsing {0}:\n{1}
hapet command must be specified. For example 'hapet build ...'
Path to the project file must be specified. For example 'hapet {0} /path/to/project.hptproj'
hapet command called {0} is not available
Error while inferencing '{0}' tag{1}
The value '{0}' could not be parsed to int
The value '{0}' is invalid for the '{1}' tag
Assembly {0} could not be found. Please check project references properly
Referenced project could not be found by path '{0}'
'{0}' could not be found
Unexpected end while parsing string literal
Unexpected declaration in struct type
Expression expected before '{0}'
Expression expected after '{0}'
Expression expected after '{0}'
Argument name expected to be an identifier
Failed to parse argument list, expected ',' or ')'
Parameter name expected to be an identifier
Param default value expected to be an expression
Expected ',' or ')/]', got '{0}'
Lambda param name expected to be an expression
Cast sub expected to be an expression
Cast target expected to be an expression
Array size expected to be an expression
Array creation requires its size or elements to be specified
Only the last array size could be not specified
Unexpected token after array creation expression
Array elements expected to be expressions
Unexpected token in array elements
Call target expected to be an expression
Call name expected to be an identifier
Failed to parse operator [..], expected ',' or ']'
At least one argument is required
Array access has to many args
Expression expected as index of an element in [..]
Declaration name expected to be an identifier
Unexpected expression after tilda (~)
Failed to parse expression, unpexpected token ({0}) {1}
Expected {0} {1}
Unexpected Token ({0}) {1}, expected {2}
Unexpected token '{0}' in expression
Identifier expected to be here
The '.' was not expected here
Identifier expected after '.'
Variable initializer has to be an expresssion
Unexpected token
This type of statement is not allowed in the scope
Attribute name expected to be here
Newline expected to be here
The right side of variable assignment expected to be an expression
Expected class name after 'class' keyword
Class name expected to be an identifier
Expression expected as 'if' statement condition
Condition of 'if' statement expected
Expression expected as 'switch' statement parameter
Condition of 'switch' statement expected
The statement expected as a 'case'
Cases expected after the 'switch' statement
Expression expected as 'case' statement parameter
Condition of 'case' statement expected
Unexpected token. Expected '=' or '\\n'
Expected return type after 'delegate' keyword
Expected delegate name after delegate return type
Delegate name expected to be an identifier
Expression expected as a type name
Unexpected token as a type name
Unexpected token after a type name
Expected enum name after 'enum' keyword
Enum name expected to be an identifier
Only one inherited type is allowed for enums
Enum field initializer expected to be an expression
This type of declaration is not allowed in enum type
Expression expected as the second parameter
Expression expected as 'while' statement parameter
Condition of 'while' loop expected
Namespace name/path expected after 'using' keyword
Namespace name/path expected after 'namespace' keyword
Unexpected token after property's 'get'
Unexpected token after property's 'set'
Keyword 'get' has to be before keyword 'set'
Property initializer expected to be an expresssion
The property expected to have 'get' keyword or body of 'set'
The property expected to have body of 'get'
The property expected to have body of 'set'
An expression expected after 'return' word
The statement after {0} has to be a declaration
Expected struct name after 'struct' keyword
Struct name expected to be an identifier
The declaration type is not allowed in struct type
Parameters expected after overloading operator
Too many parameters after overloading operator
Unexpected operator to be overloaded as unary operator
Unexpected operator to be overloaded as binary operator
Generic type name expected to be here
Generic type with the name not found
Generic property excepted to have 'get' and/or 'set' bodies
Declarations are expected in {0} body
Nested function declaration is not expected to be here
String value expected to be here
Unknown directive name
Unknown generic type constrain
Enum type itself could not be assigned to anything
Required type of the expression could not be evaluated
Array cannot has initialization values when its size is not a const
Array initialization values amount and its size are different but they have to be the same. \n You set {0} in array definition but there are {1} elements presented
The element has to be an array type
The declaration has to be 'static' or 'const' because it is declared in a 'static' class
Functions with the name that starts with 'get_' or 'set_' are not allowed
Please rename the field because a property named {0} is going to generate the field
Fields and properties cannot have the same names in a class
Function with the name is not allowed in the {0} class
Only one destructor could be declared in a class
Only one static constructor could be declared in a class/struct
The statement expected to be a declaration
The declaration type is not allowed in namespace scope
Namespace '{0}' already contains a declaration with the name
Enum type expected to be an integer
The {0} type already has an implementation of the field. Implicit shadowing is not allowed
The {0} type already has a field that {1} type has to implement for {2} interface
The {0} type does not have an implementation of {1} field
The initializer has to be compile time evaluated
The initializer has to be numeric type
Variable declaration with 'var' key has to have an initializer
Variable declaration cannot have 'void' type
Token {0} is not allowed here
The const field has to have compile time evaluated value
Undefined operator '{0}' for type {1}
Too many operators '{0}' defined for type {1}
Undefined operator '{0}' for types {1} and {2}
Too many operators '{0}' defined for types {1} and {2}
Operator '{0}' could not be used with void*
An instance of an interface or an abstract class could not be created
The declaration could not be accessed from here
The type could not be inferred
The function could not be accessed from here
Function with the name could not be found
The non-static method could only be accessed from an object
The function could not be inferred
The calling thing has to be a function or a delegate
The type of the expression has to be a class or a struct
The symbol could not be found in {0}
Array/String type expected to be indexed
Nothing could be assigned to enum field
Type of the expression expected to be boolean type
Only one 'default' case is allowed
Only constant values allowed in 'case' statements
Not enough 'return {0}' statements in function
An empty 'return' statement in function but is has to return {0}
Too many parameters were specified. Attribute has only {0} fields but You specified {1} parameters
Parameter value has to be compile time available
Parameter for the '{0}' field has to be specified because it is marked as 'Required'
Default value for the type was not found
Type {0} cannot be implicitly casted into {1}
Function name expected to be here
The expression expected to be a function
Main function could not be found
Type of the index has to be an integer type
Expression expected on the right side of assignment
Return statement with no args expected
The {0} type already has an implementation of the method. Implicit shadowing is not allowed
The {0} type already has a method {1} that {2} type has to implement for {3} interface
The {0} type does not have an implementation of {1} method
The function shadows {0} function. Implicit shadowing is not allowed
The property shadows {0} property. Implicit shadowing is not allowed
The type has to have {0} method implementation
The type has to have {0} property implementation
Abstract method cannot have body
Abstract property cannot have get/set bodies
Sealed type cannot be derived
Type {0} in interface list expected to be an interface
Type {0} cannot derive from {1} type, probably because the second one is value typed
Ambiguous user defined conversions when converting from {0} to {1}
The keyword '{0}' has to be before keyword '{1}'
Keywords '{0}' and '{1}' are ambiguous. Only one of them has to be defined
Variable declaration with 'var' key cannot have 'default' initializer
The {0} type already has an implementation of the property. Implicit shadowing is not allowed
The {0} type does not have an implementation of {1} property
The {0} method could not be found - probably because of its explicit implementation
The nested function's parent not found
The nested type's parent not found
Parent's static constructor not found
The name is ambiguous between the following functions {0} and {1}
Functions could only be called on types/objects not on {0}
Pointer type expected to be dereferenced - got {0}
The class that is used as an attribute expected to be inherited from System.Attribute class
Type of the expression could not be evaluated
Static fields and properties with no get/set blocks with polymorphic types are not allowed
Attribute class with the name could not be found
{0} is obsolete: '{1}'
(Compiler exception) The default had to be infered previously by caller
(Compiler exception) Unexpected operator {0}
(Compiler exception) The {0} type of statement is not implemented yet
(Compiler exception) AstPointerExpr could not be generated here
(Compiler exception) AstAddressOfExpr could not be generated here
(Compiler exception) The nested expr could not be generated, fatal :^(
(Compiler exception) Could not generate access code for the {0} type
(Compiler exception) Stmt could not be prepared when current step is None
(Compiler exception) There is no default value for the {0} type
(Compiler exception) There are no fields to initialize in {0}
(Compiler exception) Only one double colon (::) expected to be in the func' name - {0}
(Compiler exception) Type info could not be generated for {0}
(Compiler exception) Virtual table could not be generated for {0}
(Compiler exception) Virtual method could not be found. Has to be handled in PP
(Compiler exception) ProgramFile could not be 'null' at this point
Type {0} could not be converted to type {1} anyhow
[LLVM-validate-module] {0}
Object file could not be generated due to some errors
Constructor with specified argument types was not found in the {0} class
Call of {0} is not supported
The element '{0}' could not be accessed. You are probably trying to access a non static/const field
Loop/switch to break could not be found
Loop to continue could not be found
'DllImportAttribute' has to be specified when function is 'extern'
The 'Pack' value could not be less than 1
The 'Pack' value expected to be a power of two
Couldn't find Visual Studio binary directory
Couldn't find Visual Studio host bin directory
Couldn't find Visual Studio linker file
[LINKER] error: {0}
Failed to link
Couldn't find windows sdk
Couldn't find Visual Studio library directory
Required path {0} could not be found
Assembly {0} could not be found. Please check extern functions properly
